115200.explanation: The T@B considers it important that residents can express their opinions on important municipal themes in between elections. We support an advisory referendum.

115203.explanation: According to T@B, farmers should decide for themselves whether to allow recreational use of their own land. We also want more recreational spots ourselves; Hardinxveld is much more beautiful than we all think.

115204.explanation: Private initiatives are important. There are already enough funds available from the national government. Own initiative is appreciated and should be encouraged through positive cooperation.

115205.explanation: Large-scale distribution centers do not fit the rural character of Hardinxveld. Furthermore, we need our land more urgently for housing for our residents.

115206.explanation: The national government has saddled us with a big problem. At the same time, Sliedrecht has already built an asylum seekers' center on our municipal border without Hardinxveld's input. Such a center does not fit the culture of Hardinxveld.

115207.explanation: This way, our housing supply can remain intended for the residents of Hardinxveld-Giessendam itself.

115209.explanation: Too many rules are imposed by the national government.

115214.explanation: This is already a national obligation, especially for new construction.

115215.explanation: We will cooperate positively with permits. However, the housing construction must suit the surroundings and the landscape.

115216.explanation: As T@B, we fully support our associations. What is learned young is done old. Sports also contribute to social cohesion. Therefore, we believe, for example, that children from lower-income families should also have access to sports.

115219.explanation: Parents should finance this themselves. However, we think positively about private initiatives.

115220.explanation: We as T@B believe there should be more TALK with the associations, instead of ABOUT the associations. Moreover, sports clubs are already affected BY unnecessary rent increases, which makes membership fees more expensive.

115223.explanation: We are in favor of more police on the streets, paid by The Hague, not for enforcement officers paid from local community funds.

115224.explanation: Camera surveillance is a form of prevention. If you have nothing to hide, you don't need to worry.

115225.explanation: Unfortunately, it appears necessary in connection with domestic violence. But there must be no disclosure of whether there is a women’s shelter. Good citizenship is important in education.

115226.explanation: The Pieter de Hooghstraat project shows that the municipality can help by enabling affordable construction through lending land. We are also in favor of realizing affordable (starter) homes in cooperation with building partners and Fien Wonen.

115235.explanation: This must be done in consultation with residents. Moreover, it must not come at the expense of regular parking spaces.

115238.explanation: The price-quality ratio must be balanced.

115240.explanation: If it turns out to cost more than it yields, the dog tax must be overhauled.

115242.explanation: We think it is up to the entrepreneurs themselves. There are many options nearby to shop on Sundays.

115245.explanation: We are not in favor of prohibitions. If an entrepreneur or landowner wants this, they request a permit and it is assessed whether it fits the surroundings. The municipal council sets the framework.

115248.explanation: Primary schools need the education time primarily for reading, writing, and arithmetic. We do encourage parents to take their children to school swimming on their own initiative.

115251.explanation: It creates extra housing supply. There must be supervision and enforcement so that everything is properly done through the General Local Regulation (APV) and no nuisance arises.

115253.explanation: The center already has sufficient parking spaces, for example at Albert Heijn or the underground parking garage.

115255.explanation: Young people have it tough in Hardinxveld. Few bars and restaurants, few activities. We want Hardinxveld to remain a vibrant place now and in the future.

115256.explanation: Starting entrepreneurs deserve support from the municipality. Vacancy is a shame and causes a dull (shopping) center.

115200.explanation: The SGP is not in favor of referenda due to high costs and the fact that a simple yes or no often does not do justice to complex issues. What is important is that the elected councilors listen carefully and respond to residents' serious concerns.

115203.explanation: As far as the SGP is concerned, there are certainly possibilities for this; we actually welcome private initiatives. However, it goes too far to allow this all the time, as prior consultation about this is very important, for example with neighbors.

115204.explanation: The SGP wants to support initiatives for sustainable energy generation (e.g., on business parks) where possible, but believes the municipality should not invest public funds in this.

115205.explanation: The SGP believes that local businesses (appropriate to the scale) should primarily be given space on new and existing industrial estates.

115206.explanation: The SGP is not in favor of an asylum seekers' center on our territory, especially since there already will be one right next to our municipal border. We want to incorporate our municipal responsibilities in this regard.

115207.explanation: The SGP wants to spare the regular housing market as much as possible. Therefore, we favor housing at companies (for example in the maritime sector) provided it is humane and safe and respects the environment.

115209.explanation: Wood burning can contribute to living comfort and should therefore remain possible. We encourage reducing nuisance caused by emissions.

115214.explanation: The SGP is not in favor of unnecessary bureaucracy and government interference for entrepreneurs. Public buildings (such as the House of the Municipality) will be made even more accessible where possible.

115215.explanation: The SGP supports building more homes in our village. However, frameworks apply within which housing development can take shape.

115216.explanation: Within existing social provisions and budgets there are already sufficient opportunities to support families to enable sports participation.

115219.explanation: Culture (including music) is encouraged by the municipality. A wide range is available in society. It is up to parents and schools to make choices. The municipality does not select one specific element.

115220.explanation: Associations are very important to our village. We have no reason to believe that associations are financially collapsing. In addition, associations must be self-sustaining.

115223.explanation: Laws and rules must be enforced. Enforcement officers are necessary to monitor priorities that matter to residents, such as nuisance caused by (youth) groups and/or illegal dumping.

115224.explanation: The SGP supports increasing safety in our village and camera surveillance can contribute to this.

115225.explanation: A women's shelter is a regional facility that does not need to be established specifically in our village.

115226.explanation: The SGP genuinely wants to help first-time homebuyers. However, because starter loans drive up prices and increase debt burdens, the SGP prefers to prioritize (or offer a buyer's discount) for starters in affordable housing projects (such as at Pieter de Hooghstraat).

115235.explanation: The SGP supports the current situation.

115238.explanation: The SGP wants to keep costs in the social domain, including youth care, under control. Of course, these organizations must provide high-quality assistance.

115240.explanation: As SGP, we maintain the dog tax to continue covering expenditures related to dog ownership in the budget.

115242.explanation: The SGP sees Sunday as a collective day of rest: one day of rest in a hectic society is good for everyone. Sunday rest is widely appreciated in our village, and we want to preserve it. Therefore, shops will remain closed.

115245.explanation: The SGP is not in favor of large-scale solar fields within our municipal boundaries. Two solar fields have been approved, with which we contribute more than enough.

115248.explanation: Swimming skills are very important in a water-rich municipality like ours. Currently, there is no school swimming; if financially feasible, we would like to make this possible.

115251.explanation: The SGP wants to approach these new forms of housing comprehensively. For example, also closely considering the environment in terms of parking pressure.

115253.explanation: The SGP wants to preserve parking facilities and allow them to grow with our village. When redesigning the center, we will examine whether cars can be less visible in the streetscape.

115255.explanation: The SGP wants to retain young people in our village because without youth there is no future. The SGP wants to invest in housing projects, priority policies for new homes, and a pleasant living environment for young people and young families.

115256.explanation: The SGP does not favor such interference by the municipality. However, the municipality supports starting entrepreneurs in other ways. The municipality assists entrepreneurs through networks, by informing them about subsidies, and acts as a sparring partner.

115200.explanation: Decisions belong to the elected representatives. They have all the information and can decide based on it. Of course, participation must take place so it can be considered when making decisions.

115203.explanation: It is important that farmers generate adequate income so the land remains managed. When new methods are needed for this, we want to in principle make that possible.

115204.explanation: The success of the energy transition depends on residents and companies. It is therefore very important to involve them in sustainability and the energy transition. When financial bottlenecks arise, the ChristenUnie wants to try to find solutions.

115205.explanation: The scarce space available for business activity must be used to strengthen regional companies. This with an eye to our strength: the maritime sector and construction.

115206.explanation: Out of care for our neighbors and solidarity with other municipalities, we comply with national requests regarding shelter. We do not want to leave asylum seekers and the municipality of Ter Apel out in the cold. This is within the possibilities of our municipality's capacity.

115207.explanation: Companies working with seasonal workers must also ensure good housing outside working hours. This does not have to be on their own company premises; it must be in suitable locations.

115209.explanation: For some, open fire represents coziness, for others it means nuisance or even health risks. The ChristenUnie considers reducing nuisance from wood burning important, for example through communication about burning instructions. A total ban goes too far.

115214.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes that municipal buildings are the municipality's responsibility. Entrepreneurs are responsible for their own buildings.

115215.explanation: Spatial planning applies to everyone. Within the permitted frameworks, farmers can build houses on their land. When changes to these frameworks are necessary, the normal procedure is followed.

115216.explanation: It is important that everyone can participate in sports. If financial circumstances prevent this for people on social assistance, solutions are available, organized by the municipality or otherwise.

115219.explanation: The ChristenUnie supports talent development in children. Every child should be able to develop their talents, for example by playing sports or taking music lessons. If money is a barrier, we will solve that.

115220.explanation: Healthy associations are necessary for healthy residents. The health of an association is primarily its own responsibility. If circumstances make this difficult, the municipality can step in.

115223.explanation: Feeling safe on every street in Hardinxveld-Giessendam is of the utmost importance! Visible and accessible police are crucial for this. Supervisory officers (BOA’s) also contribute to safe streets.

115224.explanation: Feeling safe on every street in Hardinxveld-Giessendam is of the utmost importance! The ChristenUnie does not primarily think of camera surveillance. However, it can be considered in problem areas.

115225.explanation: It is important that places are available in women’s shelters. Where these places are located matters less. If initiatives arise to realize them in Hardinxveld-Giessendam, we will support them.

115226.explanation: The ChristenUnie wants a wide range of affordable and comfortable homes for starters, families, elderly people, people with disabilities, and temporary residents. For starters, a starter loan can be a solution.

115235.explanation: A sufficient number of public charging points have been realized recently. The focus must first be on further strengthening the electricity grid (solving grid congestion).

115238.explanation: Selecting the right help and thus addressing the conditions or solving the youth's problems is central. Price can then be a criterion to keep help affordable.

115240.explanation: Compared to other pets, extra costs are incurred by the municipality for dogs. Therefore, it is reasonable to collect dog tax.

115242.explanation: Sunday is a day to pray, reflect, and confess. A day to enjoy creation, family, and friends. With the different beliefs present, this day is respectful to each other. This makes Sunday special for everyone, a day to cherish!

115245.explanation: If owners/farmers come up with plans themselves, the municipality must assess these on a case-by-case basis.

115248.explanation: The ChristenUnie stands for swimming-skilled children. Every child should be swim proficient by the end of primary school. We consider this goal more important than the means. Due to transportation, school swimming is expensive and also reduces lesson time.

115251.explanation: Housing labor migrants in regular homes is not the solution to the shortage of sleeping places for them. Suitable specific accommodations must be created for labor migrants.

115253.explanation: There are already many parking spaces. The center should primarily be a pleasant area to stay in; more parking does not fit there.

115255.explanation: The ChristenUnie works towards a nice village to live in for everyone. When that succeeds, residents are more inclined to stay. For the ChristenUnie, allocating money just to keep youth in Hardinxveld-Giessendam is not an end in itself.

115256.explanation: The municipality should not enter the commercial real estate market.

115200.explanation: We believe every citizen has the right to make their voice heard.

115203.explanation: We believe this should be applied for via a permit.

115204.explanation: We support the greening of our municipality.

115205.explanation: We believe this presents a great opportunity for employment; however, it must not come at the expense of nature, traffic flow, or place excessive pressure on the energy network.

115206.explanation: Under the distribution law, we are obliged to take responsibility for a portion of the asylum seekers. We would like to collaborate with surrounding municipalities for a joint asylum seekers center.

115207.explanation: We want decent housing, also for seasonal workers. Currently, no arrangements have been made for housing these workers. We want to see clear rules on this first.

115209.explanation: Fireplaces and wood stoves have an adverse effect on our health and nature; therefore, we want to phase them out.

115214.explanation: Initially, we believe the responsibility lies with the entrepreneur, but when the capacity is lacking, we are willing to provide support where necessary.

115215.explanation: As long as the houses meet the requirements of a fully functional home, we see this as a positive step toward solving the housing shortage.

115216.explanation: Offering free sports to those on minimum income positively affects physical health and social contacts.

115219.explanation: We gladly support families experiencing financial difficulties but are not in favor of paying for every child.

115220.explanation: We consider support for sports clubs important, but of course, we want to critically assess how the money is spent.

115223.explanation: At this moment, we do not see any structural safety issues that could be resolved by community enforcement officers.

115224.explanation: If we receive signals that this is necessary, we are happy to engage in dialogue, but currently, we do not see the need.

115225.explanation: We find it important that there is a women's shelter in the region, but it does not necessarily have to be located within our municipality. We do want to support this.

115226.explanation: We want to support first-time homebuyers in securing housing.

115235.explanation: This should be realized as much as possible alongside other works to limit road break-ups.

115238.explanation: We value effectiveness more than price.

115240.explanation: Although we are open to abolishing it, there is no budget coverage for the costs the municipality incurs from dog ownership. We are open to alternatives.

115242.explanation: We are open to allowing shopping Sundays; however, we will be guided by the wishes of our municipality and the feasibility for stores, especially smaller shops that must operate with limited staff.

115245.explanation: We are not against installing solar panels; however, we want to carefully assess the desired quantity and the added value, considering grid congestion and our limited energy storage capacity.

115248.explanation: We find it important that everyone can swim.

115251.explanation: If we can make good agreements on how individual rooms are rented, we see opportunities here.

115253.explanation: We want to encourage cycling and walking; more parking spaces would encourage even more car use. At this time, we believe there are sufficient spaces but will continue to evaluate this.

115255.explanation: We favor making Hardinxveld-Giessendam generally more attractive but do not want to focus specifically on youth.

115256.explanation: We will continue to explore options as they arise.

115200.explanation: The CDA is not in favor of a referendum. The municipal council is elected to make difficult trade-offs. It is important to involve citizens in other ways on important topics.

115203.explanation: The CDA is in favor of more space for other business activities around farms.

115204.explanation: The CDA supports energy cooperatives and local residents who take initiatives to make the environment more sustainable. The municipality can support this.

115205.explanation: New companies must add value to the local economy and employment needs of current residents. The establishment of distribution centers must be assessed against this. The CDA wants to counteract the spread of commercial development.

115206.explanation: The CDA believes that an asylum seekers center should only be established if it falls under the responsibility of the distribution law. If this requirement is already met, the CDA sees no reason for an asylum seekers center.

115207.explanation: "No bed, no business." The CDA believes companies have a responsibility for seasonal workers coming to the Netherlands.

115209.explanation: A ban on the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves generates much regulatory burden and is hard to enforce. The CDA wants to reduce regulatory pressure in society.

115214.explanation: The municipality has a task to make public spaces accessible. The CDA allocates funds for this. Keeping business buildings accessible is the responsibility of the businesses themselves.

115215.explanation: The CDA wants to tackle the housing shortage, including on agricultural land, provided this does not hinder other businesses.

115216.explanation: The CDA opposes this because it entails a significant structural cost. Instead, the CDA would like a discount pass for people with tight budgets to reduce membership fees.

115219.explanation: The CDA wants sport and culture to be accessible to all children. We do this in cooperation with our associations.

115220.explanation: The CDA stands for a strong community life.

115223.explanation: The CDA wants to increase safety in our municipality. More enforcement officers (boa's) contribute to that.

115224.explanation: The CDA wants to increase safety in our municipality. Extra camera surveillance can help detect crime more effectively and quickly. It greatly assists the police in tackling crime.

115225.explanation: The CDA believes that victims of domestic violence have the right to appropriate help and support. In cooperation with the region, it can be investigated whether a women's shelter can be realized, which does not necessarily have to be located in Hardinxveld-Giessendam.

115226.explanation: The municipality must be cautious about acting as a lender, but a starter loan can provide a helpful push in the right direction.

115235.explanation: The CDA considers it important to have sufficient charging points for electric cars. These should be placed based on demand, where people with electric cars live and work. Residents should be able to submit an application to the municipality for this.

115238.explanation: Quality, effectiveness, and the best interests of the child must always come first. Responsible handling of funds is important but should never come at the expense of good and adequate youth care.

115240.explanation: The CDA wants to keep the burden on residents as low as possible.

115242.explanation: The CDA attaches importance to values and traditions, including Sunday as a day in our culture for rest, reflection, and socializing. Therefore, as far as the CDA is concerned, shops should remain closed on Sundays.

115245.explanation: The CDA strongly believes in preserving our valuable agricultural land. Therefore, we advocate first using the roofs of houses, barns, and businesses for solar energy. However, a ban goes too far.

115248.explanation: Swimming skills of our children are important. The CDA is open to initiatives for school swimming.

115251.explanation: Part of society needs room for renting out individual rooms. Think, for example, of students or young people. There must be space for this.

115253.explanation: Parking lots at Albert Heijn and Dirk van den Broek can be very busy. Therefore, we are investing extensively in parking spaces as part of the center vision.

115255.explanation: The CDA wants to invest in the youth living in the municipality. For example, by investigating whether various meeting places meet the current wishes of young people. This does not necessarily mean the municipality must spend more money to keep young people here.

115256.explanation: CDA Hardinxveld-Giessendam chooses a vibrant center but does not see the municipality buying up properties as a first step. We focus on cooperation with property owners and entrepreneurs.

statements.title: The municipality should spend more money to ensure that young people want to stay in Hardinxveld-Giessendam.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The residents of Hardinxveld-Giessendam should be able to express their opinions on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Hardinxveld-Giessendam moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: In Hardinxveld-Giessendam the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted.

titleParts.text: In Hardinxveld-Giessendam moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: More municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety should be deployed in Hardinxveld-Giessendam.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: There should be more CCTV surveillance in Hardinxveld-Giessendam.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality should allow more Sunday shopping days.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
A domestic violence shelter (women's refuge) should be established in Hardinxveld-Giessendam.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: More parking spaces should be created in the center.
The municipality should buy vacant (shop) premises in the town center and rent them to starting entrepreneurs.
